evals.name: vague-brief-triggers-intake
evals.prompt: i want to make a deck about kubernetes for non-engineers
evals.expected_output: Skill should ask intake questions (audience, technicality, goal, length, presentation style, slide density, content, source status, photos/image placeholders, assumptions, and pushback) before drafting any file. Once enough is known, produces a brainstorm HTML file under docs/brainstorms/ with the expected structure.

assertions.name: asks_intake_questions_before_drafting
assertions.description: Before writing any file, the skill asks intake questions covering audience, technicality, goal, length, presentation style, slide density, and content

assertions.name: resolves_slide_density
assertions.description: Skill resolves whether the deck should be sparse, balanced, dense executive, or leave-behind dense before synthesis

assertions.name: uses_content_aware_presentation_mode_options
assertions.description: The presentation mode question uses four options adapted to the subject, audience, goal, and setting rather than always showing the same fixed menu

assertions.name: uses_progress_labels
assertions.description: Intake turns use compact labels such as Framing 1/6, Content 2/4, or HTML brainstorm

assertions.name: produces_brainstorm_html_file
assertions.description: A -brainstorm.html file is created under docs/brainstorms/ with YYYY-MM-DD- prefix

assertions.name: html_marked_not_final
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML clearly says BRAINSTORM - NOT FINAL DECK or equivalent

assertions.name: file_has_core_idea_section
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains a concise Core idea section with proposal, why it matters, and credibility line

assertions.name: includes_clean_title_slide
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML includes a title slide as Slide 01 with title, subtitle/ask, and optional scope, but no bottom metadata row

assertions.name: header_has_no_admin_note
assertions.description: Header does not show audience, goal, review status, source status, or other admin notes under the deck title

assertions.name: does_not_expose_scaffold_sections
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML does not show intake status, audits, design principles, or builder notes unless the user asks

assertions.name: file_has_per_slide_panels
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains at least 3 slide panels focused on visible slide content

assertions.name: uses_canonical_flat_skeleton
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML follows references/wireframe-skeleton.html with section.deck directly containing article.slide-panel[data-slide] elements

assertions.name: does_not_save_text_frame_file
assertions.description: Skill does not create a separate .txt text-frame brainstorm file

assertions.name: asks_photo_placeholder_question
assertions.description: Before synthesis, the skill asks whether the user wants photos or image placeholders and records asset status

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce final .pptx, final deck .html, or .pdf output before approval. The brainstorm HTML is allowed because it is marked not final.

evals.name: rich-brief-with-reference-style
evals.prompt: Help me plan a 7-slide deck for our Q3 board review. Audience is our 8-person board - mix of operators and investors, mostly non-technical. I want them to understand why we're shifting our customer-acquisition strategy from outbound sales to product-led growth. Match the style of docs/brainstorms/2026-05-15-github-copilot-for-everyone-deck.txt - Apple/Cursor restraint, hairlines not cards, italic purple accent. Save the brainstorm to docs/brainstorms/.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes most intake questions are already answered, records the volunteered style reference as a STYLE NOTE, avoids choosing the visual theme during brainstorm, asks only missing content/source/pushback questions, and produces a 7-slide brainstorm HTML. Should not re-ask audience/goal/length.

assertions.name: skips_already_answered_intake
assertions.description: Skill does NOT re-ask audience, goal, scope, or length since all are in the prompt

assertions.name: records_style_note_without_theme_selection
assertions.description: Volunteered style/reference is recorded as a STYLE NOTE, while the brainstorm keeps theme as TBD or chosen later by html-slides

assertions.name: produces_7_slide_brainstorm
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains exactly 7 slide panels

assertions.name: uses_design_vocabulary
assertions.description: Brainstorm references hairlines, manuscript rows, italic accent word, or other terms from references/design-vocabulary.md

assertions.name: low_card_count
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML avoids nested cards and uses hairlines, spacing, and typography without selecting a final theme

assertions.name: captures_source_status_and_pushback
assertions.description: Chat recap or internal notes record Source status and informational pushback/None raised before build without cluttering the HTML

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce final .pptx, final deck .html, or .pdf output before approval. The brainstorm HTML is allowed because it is marked not final.

evals.name: different-audience-and-tone
evals.prompt: I'm presenting to a room of public school teachers next week about how AI can help with lesson planning. ~6 slides. Should feel warm and trustworthy, not cold tech vibes. Brainstorm please.
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT default to the same Apple/Cursor minimalist tone - it should adapt voice and design intent to feel warm and approachable for a teacher audience. Quick intake since most is provided, then a 6-slide brainstorm HTML.

assertions.name: adapts_voice_to_warm
assertions.description: Core idea and per-slide copy reflect a warm/approachable voice rather than default restraint

assertions.name: produces_6_slide_brainstorm
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains 6 slide panels

assertions.name: uses_teacher_relevant_examples
assertions.description: At least one slide includes examples that speak to teacher workflows

assertions.name: file_has_core_idea_section
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains a concise Core idea section

assertions.name: captures_audience_technicality
assertions.description: Audience is captured as role plus rough technicality, not only a role label

assertions.name: file_has_per_slide_panels
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML contains slide panels with low-fi wireframe previews (labeled gray placeholder boxes) and visible slide content

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce final .pptx, final deck .html, or .pdf output before approval. The brainstorm HTML is allowed because it is marked not final.

evals.name: rubber-stamped-proposal-and-later-source
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming. I want a 5-minute training deck for executives on using Codex. If asked for workflow, say ok to the suggested workflow. Say I have sample dashboard data but will show it later.
evals.expected_output: Skill should not treat rubber-stamped agent proposals or unavailable sample data as user-supplied facts. It should record accepted proposal content as ASSUMPTION, set Source status to Exists but not provided, ask informational pushback, and include a final review prompt that mentions assumptions/source status.

assertions.name: records_assumptions
assertions.description: Assumptions are tracked in the synthesis/review flow; the HTML stays focused unless the user asks to see them

assertions.name: marks_source_exists_not_provided
assertions.description: Source status is Exists but not provided when user says sample data exists but is not available yet

assertions.name: does_not_invent_source_details
assertions.description: The deck does not present unavailable sample-data schema, field names, or metrics as sourced facts

assertions.name: asks_informational_pushback
assertions.description: For training/informational deck, the skill asks whether anyone might push back before synthesis

assertions.name: approval_arc_is_detailed
assertions.description: Before asking user to approve the arc, each proposed slide includes a working title, purpose, main point, evidence/demo/source status, and how it builds to the next slide

assertions.name: approval_arc_hides_layout_design_lines
assertions.description: The user-facing Proposed Arc does not show Layout / visual design lines; it stays focused on narrative/content approval

assertions.name: internal_design_intent_uses_designer_pass
assertions.description: Before HTML generation, each content slide gets an internal DESIGN INTENT line with a named layout signature, visual protagonist, 3-5 stop scan path, and encoding choice based on content shape

assertions.name: final_review_mentions_assumptions
assertions.description: The post-brainstorm review prompt asks the user to verify assumptions and source status without putting full intake scaffolding into the HTML

evals.name: visual-html-is-canonical-brainstorm
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming for a training walkthrough deck about Codex reviewing a dashboard workflow. I want to see the brainstorm visually too.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs intake and writes docs/brainstorms/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-brainstorm.html as the canonical brainstorm artifact. It does not write a separate text-frame .txt file. The HTML is clearly marked as not final and includes workflow/artifact panels.

assertions.name: produces_canonical_html_file
assertions.description: A standalone -brainstorm.html file is created under docs/brainstorms/ and treated as the handoff artifact

assertions.name: does_not_produce_txt_brainstorm
assertions.description: No separate .txt brainstorm file is created for text-frame art

assertions.name: html_marked_not_final
assertions.description: The HTML includes wording like BRAINSTORM - NOT FINAL DECK

assertions.name: html_contains_artifact_specimens
assertions.description: The HTML includes visible workflow or artifact specimens for the training walkthrough

assertions.name: html_is_full_fidelity_monochrome
assertions.description: The HTML brainstorm renders a low-fi wireframe: a labeled gray placeholder box for each visual the DESIGN INTENT names, plus action titles, layout labels, and key bullets

assertions.name: implements_internal_design_intent
assertions.description: Each slide panel records its internal DESIGN INTENT line in the HTML comment and shows a matching labeled placeholder box

assertions.name: uses_professional_visual_choice
assertions.description: Slides choose visual forms based on the content shape, such as comparison, sequence, mechanism, relationship, evidence, product state, or decision, instead of decorating generic boxes

assertions.name: diagrams_are_real_slides
assertions.description: Useful flowcharts or workflow diagrams appear as actual slide panels in the arc, not detached sample panels

assertions.name: prevents_slide_overflow
assertions.description: Dense slide panels use fitting rules so content scales instead of clipping or overflowing

assertions.name: html_is_theme_agnostic
assertions.description: The brainstorm avoids choosing final palette, typography, template, or html-slides theme

assertions.name: handoff_uses_html
assertions.description: The skill says the brainstorm HTML file is passed to html-slides after approval

evals.name: photo-placeholders-render-in-html
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming for a 4-slide product launch deck. I want photo placeholders on the title slide and the customer story slide, no actual image files yet.
evals.expected_output: Skill records requested photo placeholders with slide/topic, what each should show, and Placeholder asset status. The brainstorm HTML renders visible .wf-box blocks (labeled with subject, intended crop, and asset status) in the relevant slide panels without inventing real images.

assertions.name: records_image_requests
assertions.description: Requested images are captured as slide/topic, visual detail, and asset status

assertions.name: renders_image_placeholders
assertions.description: Brainstorm HTML includes .wf-box blocks (labeled with subject, intended crop, and asset status) on the requested slide panels

assertions.name: does_not_invent_real_images
assertions.description: The skill does not claim a real image exists or describe unprovided image contents as sourced

assertions.name: keeps_placeholder_theme_agnostic
assertions.description: Photo placeholders reserve visual space without choosing the final html-slides theme

evals.name: compact-means-dense-executive
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming. I need a compact executive briefing for HR/L&D leaders about a gamified Copilot learning platform. By compact I mean condensed with lots of useful information in each slide, not sparse.
evals.expected_output: Skill should explicitly record Slide density as Dense executive, not interpret compact as fewer words or sparse slides. The brainstorm should use structured information-rich panels such as matrices, scorecards, operating models, decision sheets, or compact tables, and should run the strong-slide verification before presenting the HTML.

assertions.name: records_dense_executive_density
assertions.description: Synthesis or handoff records Slide density as Dense executive because the user defined compact as information-dense

assertions.name: uses_information_dense_structures
assertions.description: Dense slides use matrices, scorecards, operating models, decision sheets, compact tables, artifact strips, checklist gates, mission boards, decision cockpits, maps, or loops instead of paragraph-heavy explanations

assertions.name: avoids_sparse_compact_misread
assertions.description: Skill does not reduce the concept to sparse slides merely because the user said compact

assertions.name: runs_strong_slide_verification
assertions.description: Before presenting the brainstorm, the skill checks density match, slide job, claim title, concrete content, information design, source honesty, pushback coverage, and anti-boring design

assertions.name: runs_independent_brainstorm_review
assertions.description: Before presenting the brainstorm HTML, the skill spawns a subagent to review the saved brainstorm file against density, source honesty, visual-job variety, anti-boring design, pushback coverage, canonical structure, and overflow risk; if subagents are unavailable, it records and runs the same checklist as an explicit fallback review

assertions.name: review_prompt_mentions_density
assertions.description: Final review prompt asks the user whether the slide density matches the intended format

evals.name: avoids-boring-traditional-slide-design
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming for an executive proposal deck about a gamified Copilot learning platform. Make the brainstorm more creative and intuitive than traditional title-and-bullet slides.
evals.expected_output: Skill should use the strong slide design checklist before presenting the brainstorm. The HTML should avoid a repetitive sequence of traditional title-plus-bullet slides, name or imply distinct visual jobs across slides, and use intuitive structures such as mission board, checklist gate, decision cockpit, operating loop, artifact specimen, map surface, scorecard, or contrast wall where appropriate.

assertions.name: runs_anti_boring_checklist
assertions.description: Before presenting the brainstorm, the skill applies references/strong-slide-design-checklist.md or records an equivalent anti-boring design check

assertions.name: runs_independent_brainstorm_review
assertions.description: Before presenting the brainstorm HTML, the skill spawns a subagent to review the saved brainstorm file against density, source honesty, visual-job variety, anti-boring design, pushback coverage, canonical structure, and overflow risk; if subagents are unavailable, it records and runs the same checklist as an explicit fallback review

assertions.name: uses_named_visual_jobs
assertions.description: Content slides have clear visual jobs such as map, loop, decision, contrast, artifact, proof, scorecard, checklist, specimen, or ask

assertions.name: internal_design_pass_before_html
assertions.description: The skill performs detailed layout / visual design internally after arc approval and before HTML generation starts

assertions.name: technical_slides_use_infographic_boards_when_needed
assertions.description: Technical mechanism slides name a rich visual (technical infographic board, node-edge loop, matrix, formula, state legend) in their DESIGN INTENT and show it as a labeled placeholder box, not a simple title-plus-bullet panel

assertions.name: visual_design_has_protagonist_scan_path_encoding
assertions.description: The internal DESIGN INTENT and resulting HTML make the first eye stop, 3-5 stop scan path, and visual encoding clear

assertions.name: avoids_adjacent_repetition
assertions.description: Adjacent content slides do not use the same layout signature repeatedly

assertions.name: includes_intuitive_artifacts
assertions.description: At least one slide shows a concrete artifact, workflow, checklist, mission board, decision surface, or specimen rather than abstract bullets

assertions.name: uses_real_renderer_when_named
assertions.description: If the brainstorm names Mermaid, React Flow, D3, or another renderer, the HTML actually imports and renders that library with a pinned CDN URL, a documented reason, and a fallback; Mermaid is preferred for quick brainstorm flowcharts

assertions.name: checks_diagram_readability
assertions.description: Auto-laid diagrams keep node labels short enough to read after rendering, with detail moved into adjacent slide content when needed

assertions.name: keeps_source_honesty
assertions.description: More creative visuals do not hide assumptions, placeholders, or missing evidence

evals.name: presentation-mode-options-adapt-to-content
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming. I need to propose a gamified Copilot learning platform to executives. It should help employees learn Copilot Chat and Copilot inside Teams, Outlook, SharePoint, PowerPoint, and related apps.
evals.expected_output: The presentation mode question should offer content-aware options such as executive decision briefing, product demo narrative, training walkthrough, and leave-behind/async deck. It should not use a rigid generic menu where compact executive briefing overlaps with slide density. Slide density should be asked separately after the mode.

assertions.name: q5_options_are_contextual
assertions.description: Question 5 presentation mode options are tailored to the gamified Copilot platform proposal and executive audience

assertions.name: q5_separates_usage_from_density
assertions.description: Question 5 labels the usage/narrative mode without baking in density words such as compact or dense

assertions.name: records_visible_mode_and_normalized_style
assertions.description: The recap records the user-facing presentation mode plus a normalized presentation style/category for handoff

assertions.name: q6_handles_density_separately
assertions.description: The skill still asks slide density separately after presentation mode and uses the answer to shape slide content

evals.name: single-slide-proposal-uses-decision-tree
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming. I want to create a slide to propose how department managers can promote AI usage in the company.
evals.expected_output: Skill should route into proposal sharpener mode rather than generic deck intake. It should ask one dependency-resolving question at a time with a recommended answer, resolve audience, desired action, blocker, mechanism, manager ask, success measure, examples, slide structure, and takeaway line, then produce or hand off an approved one-slide proposal brief without inflating it into a multi-slide deck.

assertions.name: uses_proposal_sharpener_mode
assertions.description: The skill recognizes this as a proposal/adoption/single-slide request and uses a decision-tree proposal flow

assertions.name: asks_one_question_at_a_time
assertions.description: The interaction asks one dependency-resolving question per turn instead of batching generic deck intake

assertions.name: provides_recommended_answer
assertions.description: Each proposal-sharpening question includes a recommended answer plus a short reason

assertions.name: resolves_proposal_spine
assertions.description: The final brief resolves audience, desired action, blocker/tension, program mechanism, specific ask, success measure, examples/proof, slide structure, and takeaway line

assertions.name: preserves_single_slide_constraint
assertions.description: If the user confirms a standalone slide, the skill does not expand the request into a generic multi-slide deck

assertions.name: one_slide_brief_is_handoff_ready
assertions.description: The approved one-slide proposal brief is specific enough for html-slides to build: title, core message, proposal, ask, success measure, examples, structure, and takeaway

assertions.name: tracks_assumptions_for_agent_proposals
assertions.description: If the user accepts agent-proposed content, the skill records it as ASSUMPTION unless the user supplied the facts or wording

evals.name: persuasion-rigor-path-budget-approval
evals.prompt: I need a deck to get budget approval from our VP of Engineering for rolling out an internal AI code-review tool. She is skeptical about AI tooling. I have results from a 3-week pilot with one team (review turnaround dropped from 26h to 9h), but no company-wide data yet.
evals.expected_output: Skill classifies the deck as persuasion (Goal = decision/approval), runs the two-part evidence intake, records the VP's strongest objection verbatim, proposes an answer-first arc with the ask on slide 2, and produces a brainstorm HTML where every panel carries an ARGUMENT comment, every stat carries SOURCES or ASSUMPTION, the five-check rigor audit is printed as a RIGOR AUDIT comment with all PASS (or user-waived findings), and the titles-only read-through retells the argument and the ask.

assertions.name: classifies_persuasion_and_runs_evidence_intake
assertions.description: The deck is classified as a persuasion deck from Goal = decision/approval, and the sharpened two-part evidence intake is asked one question at a time: hardest evidence first, then the strongest objection

assertions.name: captures_objection_verbatim
assertions.description: The skeptical VP's strongest objection is captured verbatim during intake, not paraphrased into something weaker

assertions.name: synthesis_includes_stress_test_lines
assertions.description: The Phase 2 synthesis includes both 'Strongest objection' and 'How the deck answers it' lines, and the named rebutting slide actually exists in the proposed arc

assertions.name: answer_first_arc_for_decision_deck
assertions.description: The proposed arc is answer-first: the core message and the budget ask appear on slide 2, and the closing slide restates rather than introduces the ask

assertions.name: argument_block_on_every_panel
assertions.description: Every slide panel in the brainstorm HTML carries an ARGUMENT comment - either CLAIM + EVIDENCE + SOURCES or ROLE: structural - with no blank or placeholder EVIDENCE

assertions.name: rigor_audit_printed_and_resolved
assertions.description: The five-check RIGOR AUDIT comment is printed in the brainstorm HTML and every check is PASS or explicitly accepted by the user on the record

assertions.name: partial_evidence_not_inflated
assertions.description: The single-team pilot result is presented with its scope stated (one team, 3 weeks); missing company-wide data is framed as a proposal or ASSUMPTION, never invented or generalized into proof

assertions.name: titles_only_readthrough_performed
assertions.description: The titles-only read-through check is performed: the slide titles read in order retell the argument and the ask, with no duplicate claims or gaps
